purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.body:
## Purpose

Use this pack for music, writing, design, visual art, video, worldbuilding, and other creative projects.

The pack exists to preserve direction, taste, references, iteration history, and release readiness.

recommended_files.title: Recommended Files
recommended_files.body:
## Recommended Files

- `DOCS.json`
- `STATE.md`
- `DIRECTION.md`
- `REFERENCES.md`
- `WORKS.md`
- `FEEDBACK.md`
- `DECISIONS.md`
- `RELEASE.md`
- `JOURNAL.md`
- `PIPELINE-CONFIG.json`

Read `DOCS.json` before reading, creating, renaming, or updating project documents so the agent chooses the correct source and write target.

creative_defaults.title: Creative Defaults
creative_defaults.body:
## Creative Defaults

- Keep the current creative target explicit.
- Research known creative workflows, reference systems, genre conventions, production archetypes, and borrowed release/review practices before inventing a custom process.
- Treat new requests, suggestions, and problem reports as steer-direction checkpoints: first ask whether the requested path protects the creative direction, reduces future friction, and follows proven production or review practice.
- Prefer borrowed or adapted practices when they preserve taste while reducing process friction.
- Separate taste decisions from technical execution.
- Preserve references and comparison points.
- Use SocratexAI JSON tools for agent-only structured JSON files when practical: `doc_read`, `doc_keys`, `doc_item_insert`, `doc_item_move`, and `doc_item_migrate`.
- Keep user-facing creative planning files in Markdown unless the user asks for JSON.
- Run document audit after changing pipeline or agent-managed memory when practical.
- Version important outputs.
- Record feedback without automatically obeying it.
- Prefer concrete artifacts over abstract planning.
- Treat dissatisfaction as useful data when it names a contrast, missing quality, or unwanted effect.

review_gate.title: Review Gate
review_gate.body:
## Review Gate

Check:

- Does the work match the intended direction?
- Are references visible and relevant?
- Is feedback specific enough to act on?
- Is the current version shippable, exploratory, or discarded?
- Is the next iteration focused on one meaningful improvement?
